title: New York City 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: New York City 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to New York City, United States: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Empire State Building Observatory and Top of the Rock (Rockefeller Center). Prices from $2536 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering New York City, United States — Four-season humid continental climate with hot, humid summers (Jun-Aug), cold winters with occasional snow (Dec-Feb), and mild, colorful spring and autumn shoulder seasons. April to June and September to early November for mild, comfortable weather and fewer crowds than summer. This itinerary blends Empire State Building Observatory, Top of the Rock (Rockefeller Center), Central Park with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Empire State Building Observatory, Top of the Rock (Rockefeller Center), Central Park
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in New York City
itinerary.description: Land at John F. Kennedy International Airport (JFK) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Empire State Building Observatory — Art Deco skyscraper observation decks. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: New York City: Top of the Rock (Rockefeller Center) & One World Observatory
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Top of the Rock (Rockefeller Center) — Observation deck with Empire State Building views. In the afternoon, continue to One World Observatory — Observatory atop One World Trade Center. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: New York City: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island ferry & The Metropolitan Museum of Art
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island ferry — Ferry access to both landmark islands. In the afternoon, continue to The Metropolitan Museum of Art — One of the world's largest art museums. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island ferry

itinerary.title: Day Trip to The Hamptons
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to The Hamptons, a popular side trip from New York City. Return to New York City in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include The Cloisters museum and gardens — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: New York City: MoMA (Museum of Modern Art) & 9/11 Memorial & Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to MoMA (Museum of Modern Art) — Modern and contemporary art collection. In the afternoon, continue to 9/11 Memorial & Museum — Museum and memorial pools at Ground Zero. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Hudson Valley
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Hudson Valley, a popular side trip from New York City. Return to New York City in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: New York City: Edge Observation Deck (Hudson Yards) & Broadway show ticket
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Edge Observation Deck (Hudson Yards) — Outdoor sky deck with a glass floor. In the afternoon, continue to Broadway show ticket — A live Broadway theatre performance. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Roosevelt Island Tram — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Philadelphia
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Philadelphia, a popular side trip from New York City. Return to New York City in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: New York City: American Museum of Natural History & Central Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to American Museum of Natural History — Dinosaur fossils and planetarium. In the afternoon, continue to Central Park — 843-acre park in the heart of Manhattan. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: New York City: Times Square & Brooklyn Bridge
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Times Square — Iconic neon-lit commercial intersection. In the afternoon, continue to Brooklyn Bridge — Historic pedestrian walkway between boroughs. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Green-Wood Cemetery — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to The High Line or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to JFK for your departure flight.

travelTips: Tap your contactless card or phone directly on OMNY subway readers
Book Broadway tickets and popular museum timed-entry slots in advance
Walk briskly and stay alert to avoid blocking sidewalk or subway stair traffic
Check for pay-what-you-wish hours at major museums
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Comfortable shoes for extensive walking on hard pavement
Layered clothing for unpredictable weather across seasons
A warm coat and waterproof boots for winter snow and slush
A compact umbrella for sudden showers
thingsToDo: Statue of Liberty & Ellis Island ferry
thingsToAvoid: Buying "discount" Broadway or attraction tickets from street vendors near Times Square
Standing in the middle of subway stairs or sidewalks during rush hour
Hailing a cab instead of the subway for cross-town trips in heavy traffic
Walking through Central Park alone late at night
topRestaurants: Le Bernardin — French fine dining, seafood ($$$$)
Russ & Daughters — Jewish appetizing, smoked fish ($$)
Xi'an Famous Foods — Chinese hand-pulled noodles ($)
nightlife: Blue Note jazz club, Greenwich Village
instagramSpots: Top of the Rock skyline view
luxuryExperiences: Front-row Broadway tickets with a backstage tour
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset cocktails at a rooftop bar overlooking the skyline
Horse-drawn carriage ride through Central Park
budgetExperiences: Free Staten Island Ferry ride past the Statue of Liberty
Walk the Brooklyn Bridge and High Line

festivalCalendar.name: New Year's Eve Ball Drop, Times Square

localEtiquette: Walk briskly and stay to the right on sidewalks and subway stairs.
Tipping 18-20% is standard at restaurants and for taxi rides.
Let subway passengers exit before boarding.
Personal space is limited on transit — crowding isn't personal.
Direct, efficient communication is the local norm.
localTransportGuide: The subway runs 24/7 and is the fastest way around the city — tap a contactless card or phone directly on an OMNY reader, no separate MetroCard needed. Yellow cabs and rideshares (Uber/Lyft) are plentiful but often slower than the subway in Manhattan traffic.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to early November for mild, comfortable weather and fewer crowds than summer.
weather: Four-season humid continental climate with hot, humid summers (Jun-Aug), cold winters with occasional snow (Dec-Feb), and mild, colorful spring and autumn shoulder seasons.
visaInformation: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries need an approved ESTA before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa.
averageFlightTime: ~13.9h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit New York City?
faqs.answer: Travelers from Visa Waiver Program countries need an approved ESTA before flying, valid for stays up to 90 days; other nationalities require a B-2 tourist visa.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit New York City?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to early November for mild, comfortable weather and fewer crowds than summer. Four-season humid continental climate with hot, humid summers (Jun-Aug), cold winters with occasional snow (Dec-Feb), and mild, colorful spring and autumn shoulder seasons.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is New York City safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: New York City is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to New York City?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Nadia Ibrahim
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: New York City is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially The Metropolitan Museum of Art. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Kim
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Edge Observation Deck (Hudson Yards) alone was worth the trip, but combined with Washington Square Park this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Nadia Larsen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: New York City is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Edge Observation Deck (Hudson Yards). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Cohen
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: MoMA (Museum of Modern Art) alone was worth the trip, but combined with The Metropolitan Museum of Art this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Tom Botha
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond St. Patrick's Cathedral and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Liam Santos
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Our trip to New York City was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Staten Island Ferry was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
